# services.dawarich

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.dawarich`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.dawarich.automaticMigrations` | `boolean` | Whether to perform database migrations automatically |
| `services.dawarich.configureNginx` | `boolean` | Configure nginx as a reverse proxy for dawarich. Alternatively you can configure a reverse-proxy of your choice to serve these paths: `/ -> ${pkgs.dawarich}/public` `/ -> 127.0.0.1:{{ webPort }} `(If there was no file in the directory above.) Make sure that websockets are forwarded properly. You might want to set up caching of some requests. Take a look at dawarich's provided reverse proxy configurations at `https://dawarich.app/docs/tutorials/reverse-proxy`. |
| `services.dawarich.database.createLocally` | `boolean` | Whether to configure a local PostgreSQL server and database for Dawarich. The connection is performed via Unix sockets. |
| `services.dawarich.database.host` | `string` | Hostname or address of the postgresql server. If an absolute path is given here, it will be interpreted as a unix socket path. |
| `services.dawarich.database.name` | `string` | The name of the dawarich database. |
| `services.dawarich.database.passwordFile` | `null or absolute path` | A file containing the password corresponding to {option}`services.dawarich.database.user`. |
| `services.dawarich.database.port` | `null or 16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | Port of the postgresql server. |
| `services.dawarich.database.user` | `string` | The database user for dawarich. |
| `services.dawarich.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Dawarich, a self-hostable alternative to Google Location History. |
| `services.dawarich.environment` | `attribute set of (null or string or absolute path or package)` | Extra environment variables to pass to all dawarich services. |
| `services.dawarich.extraEnvFiles` | `list of absolute path` | Extra environment files to pass to all Dawarich services. Useful for passing down environment secrets. |
| `services.dawarich.group` | `string` | Group under which dawarich runs. |
| `services.dawarich.localDomain` | `string` | The domain serving your Dawarich instance. |
| `services.dawarich.package` | `package` | The dawarich package to use. |
| `services.dawarich.redis.createLocally` | `boolean` | Whether to configure a local Redis server for Dawarich. The connection is performed via Unix sockets by default, but that can be changed by configuring {option}`services.dawarich.redis.host` and {option}`services.dawarich.redis.port`. |
| `services.dawarich.redis.host` | `string` | The redis host Dawarich will connect to. |
| `services.dawarich.redis.port` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | The port of the redis server Dawarich will connect to. Set to zero to disable TCP and use Unix sockets instead. |
| `services.dawarich.secretKeyBaseFile` | `null or string` | Path to file containing the secret key base. A new secret key base can be generated by running: `nix build -f '<nixpkgs>' dawarich; cd result; bin/bundle exec rails secret` This file is loaded using systemd credentials, and therefore does not need to be owned by the dawarich user. If this option is null, it will be created at /var/lib/dawarich/secrets/secret-key-base with a new secret key base. |
| `services.dawarich.sidekiqProcesses` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | How many Sidekiq processes should be used to handle background jobs, and which job classes they handle. Can be used to [speed up](https://dawarich.app/docs/FAQ/#how-to-speed-up-the-import-process) the import process. |
| `services.dawarich.sidekiqProcesses.<name>.jobClasses` | `list of (one of "app_version_checking", "archival", "cache", "data_migrations", "default", "digests", "exports", "families", "imports", "mailers", "places", "points", "reverse_geocoding", "stats", "tracks", "trips", "visit_suggesting")` | If not empty, which job classes should be executed by this process. *If left empty, all job classes will be executed by this process.* |
| `services.dawarich.sidekiqProcesses.<name>.threads` | `null or signed integer` | Number of threads this process should use for executing jobs. If null, the configured `sidekiqThreads` are used. |
| `services.dawarich.sidekiqThreads` | `signed integer` | Worker threads used by the dawarich-sidekiq-all service. If `sidekiqProcesses` is configured and any processes specify null `threads`, this value is used. |
| `services.dawarich.smtp.fromAddress` | `null or string` | "From" address used when sending emails to users. |
| `services.dawarich.smtp.host` | `null or string` | SMTP host used when sending emails to users. |
| `services.dawarich.smtp.passwordFile` | `null or absolute path` | Path to file containing the SMTP password. |
| `services.dawarich.smtp.port` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | SMTP port used when sending emails to users. |
| `services.dawarich.smtp.user` | `null or string` | SMTP login name. |
| `services.dawarich.user` | `string` | User under which dawarich runs. If it is set to "dawarich", that user will be created, otherwise it should be set to the name of a user created elsewhere. |
| `services.dawarich.webPort` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | TCP port used by the dawarich web service. |
